role: Iranian nuclear physicist; professor at Shahid Beheshti University
bio: Majid Shahriari was an Iranian nuclear physicist and professor at [Shahid Beheshti University](/orgs/shahid-beheshti-university) in Tehran. He was involved in Iran's civilian nuclear programme, working on reactor physics and neutron transport calculations. On 29 November 2010, Shahriari was killed in a car-bombing in Tehran — a bomb was attached to his vehicle by a motorcyclist while he was driving his wife to work. His wife survived. The same morning, [Fereydoun Abbasi-Davani](/people/fereydoun-abbasi-davani) survived a similar attack in a separate part of Tehran. The coordinated attacks were part of a broader campaign of targeted killings against Iranian nuclear scientists between 2010 and 2012, widely attributed to Israeli intelligence (Mossad). Shahriari's killing was one of the most significant in this series; Iran's government accused the United States and Israel.

sources.title: Iran Says Nuclear Scientist Killed in Explosion

editorial_notes: Killed 29 November 2010. Same day as the attack on Fereydoun Abbasi-Davani. Both attacks were car-bombings by motorcyclists.
